ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE TO ALL COUNSEL SET FOR JUL-07-2011 IN DEPARTMENT 610 AT 10:30 AM FOR FAILURE TO COMPLETE MEDIATION. THE JUN-22-2011 MEDIATION ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE IS OFF CALENDAR. NOTICE SENT BY COURT.

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT GILBERT LEE'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR ALTERNATIVELY SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED. DEFENDANT FAILED TO MAINTAIN ITS SUMMARY JUDGMENT BURDEN. ISSUES RESOLVED IN THE UNDERLYING UNLAWFUL DETAINER ACTION BEFORE JUDGE STEWART ARE UNRELATED TO THE REMAINING CAUSES OF ACTION IN THIS CASE. MOREOVER, THE COURT REJECTS DEFENDANT'S ARGUMENTS REGARDING THE DETERMINATION OF WHETHER A DUTY EXISTED BECAUSE THERE IS NO SUPPORTING EVIDENCE REGARDING THAT ISSUE. FURTHER, PLAINTIFFS' OBJECTION TO DEFENDANT'S REFERENCE TO THE HEARING TRANSCRIPT OF THE UNLAWFUL DETAINER ACTION WITHOUT CITATION TO HIS SEPARATE STATEMENT IS SUSTAINED. DEFENDANT'S FAILURE TO SET FORTH FACTS AND STATEMENTS OF THE COURT DURING THE UNLAWFUL DETAINER TRIAL IN ITS SEPARATE STATEMENT VIOLATES THE PROCEDURAL REQUIREMENTS UNDER CCP SECTION 437(B)(1). PLAINTIFF TO PREPARE A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: LORETTA M. GIORGI, REPORTER: KARLA ELLIS-DAVIS, CSR #12998
SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE HELD, CASE NOT SETTLED. TRIAL DATE REMAINS. JUDGE JOHN K. STEWART, NOT REPORTED, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA (505).

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jun-23-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANT GILBERT LEE'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR ALTERNATIVELY SUMMARY ADJUDICATION.

APPEARANCES: Robert Salinas, Esq. of Sundeen Salinas & Pyle 510/ 663-9240 & Philip O'Brien, Esq. 827-4605 for pltfs & opposing parties; David Semel, Esq. of Haapala, Thompson & Abern, LLP 510/763-2324 for deft & moving party.

RULING: Argued and the Court took the matter under submission.
After hearing, the Court ruled as follows: Tentative ruling adopted - DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/ADJUDICATION IS DENIED. DEFENDANT FAILED TO MAINTAIN ITS SUMMARY JUDGMENT BURDEN. ISSUES RESOLVED IN THE UNDERLYING UNLAWFUL DETAINER ACTION BEFORE JUDGE STEWART ARE UNRELATED TO THE REMAINING CAUSES OF ACTION IN THIS CASE. MOREOVER, THE COURT REJECTS DEFENDANT'S ARGUMENTS REGARDING THE DETERMINATION OF WHETHER A DUTY EXISTED BECAUSE THERE IS NO SUPPORTING EVIDENCE REGARDING THAT ISSUE.
Further, plaintiffs' objection to defendant's reference to the hearing transcript of the unlawful detainer action without citation to his separate statement is sustained. Defendant's failure to set forth facts and statements of the Court during the unlawful detainer trial in its separate statement violates the procedural requirements under CCP section 437(b)(1). Plaintiff to prepare a proposed form of order. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998
